reasoning: HD's short-term momentum has been mildly negative, dropping from $400 in early Feb 2025 to ~$354 by early May. The yfinance statistical trend analysis model estimates approximately a 45% chance of the stock being higher by the 1-week mark (May 18). Thus, I set the Date 1 estimate slightly below 50% at 46%. Q1 earnings on May 20 will introduce volatility for Date 2 (1 month horizon); here, positive equity drift and potential earnings surprises begin to balance out the recent downtrend, leading to an estimate of 48%. For longer horizons (Dates 3 and 4 at 3 and 6 months out), the historical equity risk premium and typical positive drift of large-cap stocks start to dominate short-term noise and current momentum factors. This pushes the probabilities gradually over the 50% mark to 51% and 53%, respectively. This schedule stays close to 0.5 overall, reflecting the near-unpredictable random walk nature of stocks while accounting for initial negative momentum that reverts towards positive drift over extended periods.
